  5. Tidyverse and ggplot2 Analysis with Gapminder Dataset

Tidyverse and ggplot2 Analysis with Gapminder Dataset

Author

Data Analysis with R

Published

July 7, 2025

[[1]]
[1] "rmarkdown" "stats"     "graphics"  "grDevices" "datasets"  "utils"    
[7] "methods"   "base"     

[[2]]
[1] "gapminder" "rmarkdown" "stats"     "graphics"  "grDevices" "datasets" 
[7] "utils"     "methods"   "base"     

[[3]]
 [1] "lubridate" "forcats"   "stringr"   "dplyr"     "purrr"     "readr"    
 [7] "tidyr"     "tibble"    "ggplot2"   "tidyverse" "gapminder" "rmarkdown"
[13] "stats"     "graphics"  "grDevices" "datasets"  "utils"     "methods"  
[19] "base"     

[[4]]
 [1] "readxl"    "lubridate" "forcats"   "stringr"   "dplyr"     "purrr"    
 [7] "readr"     "tidyr"     "tibble"    "ggplot2"   "tidyverse" "gapminder"
[13] "rmarkdown" "stats"     "graphics"  "grDevices" "datasets"  "utils"    
[19] "methods"   "base"     

[[5]]
 [1] "openxlsx"  "readxl"    "lubridate" "forcats"   "stringr"   "dplyr"    
 [7] "purrr"     "readr"     "tidyr"     "tibble"    "ggplot2"   "tidyverse"
[13] "gapminder" "rmarkdown" "stats"     "graphics"  "grDevices" "datasets" 
[19] "utils"     "methods"   "base"

 [1] "gridExtra" "openxlsx"  "readxl"    "lubridate" "forcats"   "stringr"  
 [7] "dplyr"     "purrr"     "readr"     "tidyr"     "tibble"    "ggplot2"  
[13] "tidyverse" "gapminder" "rmarkdown" "stats"     "graphics"  "grDevices"
[19] "datasets"  "utils"     "methods"   "base"     

[[7]]
 [1] "ggExtra"   "gridExtra" "openxlsx"  "readxl"    "lubridate" "forcats"  
 [7] "stringr"   "dplyr"     "purrr"     "readr"     "tidyr"     "tibble"   
[13] "ggplot2"   "tidyverse" "gapminder" "rmarkdown" "stats"     "graphics" 
[19] "grDevices" "datasets"  "utils"     "methods"   "base"     

[[8]]
 [1] "knitr"     "ggExtra"   "gridExtra" "openxlsx"  "readxl"    "lubridate"
 [7] "forcats"   "stringr"   "dplyr"     "purrr"     "readr"     "tidyr"    
[13] "tibble"    "ggplot2"   "tidyverse" "gapminder" "rmarkdown" "stats"    
[19] "graphics"  "grDevices" "datasets"  "utils"     "methods"   "base"

Introduction

This R Markdown document demonstrates data manipulation and visualization using the tidyverse suite of packages, particularly dplyr, tidyr, readr, stringr, and ggplot2. We will use the gapminder dataset to perform various data operations and create visualizations. The document also includes solutions to the provided exercises.

Installing and Loading Packages

First, we load the necessary packages using renv.

library(renv)
renv::restore(prompt = FALSE)

Exploring the Gapminder Dataset

Let’s take a look at the first few rows of the gapminder dataset.

# View first six rows
head(gapminder) 
# A tibble: 6 × 6
  country     continent  year lifeExp      pop gdpPercap
  <fct>       <fct>     <int>   <dbl>    <int>     <dbl>
1 Afghanistan Asia       1952    28.8  8425333      779.
2 Afghanistan Asia       1957    30.3  9240934      821.
3 Afghanistan Asia       1962    32.0 10267083      853.
4 Afghanistan Asia       1967    34.0 11537966      836.
5 Afghanistan Asia       1972    36.1 13079460      740.
6 Afghanistan Asia       1977    38.4 14880372      786.

Data Manipulation with dplyr

The dplyr package provides functions for data manipulation, such as select(), filter(), mutate(), arrange(), summarise(), and group_by().

Selecting Columns

Select specific columns using select().

individual columns

# Select individual columns
sel_data <- gapminder %>% select(country, continent, pop)
head(sel_data)
# A tibble: 6 × 3
  country     continent      pop
  <fct>       <fct>        <int>
1 Afghanistan Asia       8425333
2 Afghanistan Asia       9240934
3 Afghanistan Asia      10267083
4 Afghanistan Asia      11537966
5 Afghanistan Asia      13079460
6 Afghanistan Asia      14880372

range of columns

# Select range of columns
sel_data <- gapminder %>% select(country, year:pop)
head(sel_data)
# A tibble: 6 × 4
  country      year lifeExp      pop
  <fct>       <int>   <dbl>    <int>
1 Afghanistan  1952    28.8  8425333
2 Afghanistan  1957    30.3  9240934
3 Afghanistan  1962    32.0 10267083
4 Afghanistan  1967    34.0 11537966
5 Afghanistan  1972    36.1 13079460
6 Afghanistan  1977    38.4 14880372

Exclude columns

# Exclude columns
sel_data <- gapminder %>% select(-pop)
head(sel_data)
# A tibble: 6 × 5
  country     continent  year lifeExp gdpPercap
  <fct>       <fct>     <int>   <dbl>     <dbl>
1 Afghanistan Asia       1952    28.8      779.
2 Afghanistan Asia       1957    30.3      821.
3 Afghanistan Asia       1962    32.0      853.
4 Afghanistan Asia       1967    34.0      836.
5 Afghanistan Asia       1972    36.1      740.
6 Afghanistan Asia       1977    38.4      786.

Exclude multiple columns

# Exclude multiple columns
sel_data <- gapminder %>% select(-pop, -country)
head(sel_data)
# A tibble: 6 × 4
  continent  year lifeExp gdpPercap
  <fct>     <int>   <dbl>     <dbl>
1 Asia       1952    28.8      779.
2 Asia       1957    30.3      821.
3 Asia       1962    32.0      853.
4 Asia       1967    34.0      836.
5 Asia       1972    36.1      740.
6 Asia       1977    38.4      786.

Exclude a vector of columns

# Exclude a vector of columns
sel_data <- gapminder %>% select(-c(pop, country))
head(sel_data)
# A tibble: 6 × 4
  continent  year lifeExp gdpPercap
  <fct>     <int>   <dbl>     <dbl>
1 Asia       1952    28.8      779.
2 Asia       1957    30.3      821.
3 Asia       1962    32.0      853.
4 Asia       1967    34.0      836.
5 Asia       1972    36.1      740.
6 Asia       1977    38.4      786.

Exclude a Complement of columns

# Complement of columns
f1 <- gapminder %>% select(!c(continent, gdpPercap))
head(f1)
# A tibble: 6 × 4
  country      year lifeExp      pop
  <fct>       <int>   <dbl>    <int>
1 Afghanistan  1952    28.8  8425333
2 Afghanistan  1957    30.3  9240934
3 Afghanistan  1962    32.0 10267083
4 Afghanistan  1967    34.0 11537966
5 Afghanistan  1972    36.1 13079460
6 Afghanistan  1977    38.4 14880372

Rename columns with select

# Rename columns with select
sel_data <- gapminder %>% select(Life_Expectancy = lifeExp)
head(sel_data)
# A tibble: 6 × 1
  Life_Expectancy
            <dbl>
1            28.8
2            30.3
3            32.0
4            34.0
5            36.1
6            38.4

Rename columns with rename()

# Rename columns with rename()
sel_data <- gapminder %>% rename(Life_Expectancy = lifeExp)
head(sel_data)
# A tibble: 6 × 6
  country     continent  year Life_Expectancy      pop gdpPercap
  <fct>       <fct>     <int>           <dbl>    <int>     <dbl>
1 Afghanistan Asia       1952            28.8  8425333      779.
2 Afghanistan Asia       1957            30.3  9240934      821.
3 Afghanistan Asia       1962            32.0 10267083      853.
4 Afghanistan Asia       1967            34.0 11537966      836.
5 Afghanistan Asia       1972            36.1 13079460      740.
6 Afghanistan Asia       1977            38.4 14880372      786.

Additional Selection Methods

Select columns using indices, ranges, or specific patterns.

Select by index

# Select by index
gapminder %>% select(c(2, 3)) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 2
  continent  year
  <fct>     <int>
1 Asia       1952
2 Asia       1957
3 Asia       1962
4 Asia       1967
5 Asia       1972
6 Asia       1977

Select by index range

# Select by index range
gapminder %>% select(2:3) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 2
  continent  year
  <fct>     <int>
1 Asia       1952
2 Asia       1957
3 Asia       1962
4 Asia       1967
5 Asia       1972
6 Asia       1977

Exclude by labels

# Select by labels
gapminder %>% select(c('country', 'continent')) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 2
  country     continent
  <fct>       <fct>    
1 Afghanistan Asia     
2 Afghanistan Asia     
3 Afghanistan Asia     
4 Afghanistan Asia     
5 Afghanistan Asia     
6 Afghanistan Asia

Exclude by labels

# Exclude by labels
gapminder %>% select(-c('country', 'continent')) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 4
   year lifeExp      pop gdpPercap
  <int>   <dbl>    <int>     <dbl>
1  1952    28.8  8425333      779.
2  1957    30.3  9240934      821.
3  1962    32.0 10267083      853.
4  1967    34.0 11537966      836.
5  1972    36.1 13079460      740.
6  1977    38.4 14880372      786.

Select columns ending with ‘p’

# Select columns starting with 'co'
gapminder %>% select(starts_with('co')) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 2
  country     continent
  <fct>       <fct>    
1 Afghanistan Asia     
2 Afghanistan Asia     
3 Afghanistan Asia     
4 Afghanistan Asia     
5 Afghanistan Asia     
6 Afghanistan Asia

Select columns ending with ‘p’

# Select columns ending with 'p'
gapminder %>% select(ends_with('p')) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 3
  lifeExp      pop gdpPercap
    <dbl>    <int>     <dbl>
1    28.8  8425333      779.
2    30.3  9240934      821.
3    32.0 10267083      853.
4    34.0 11537966      836.
5    36.1 13079460      740.
6    38.4 14880372      786.

# Select columns containing ‘pop’

# Select columns containing 'pop'
gapminder %>% select(contains('pop')) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 1
       pop
     <int>
1  8425333
2  9240934
3 10267083
4 11537966
5 13079460
6 14880372

Filtering Rows

Use filter() to subset rows based on conditions.

Filter for Belgium

# Filter for Belgium
filt_data <- gapminder %>% filter(country == "Belgium") %>% head(8)
filt_data
# A tibble: 8 × 6
  country continent  year lifeExp     pop gdpPercap
  <fct>   <fct>     <int>   <dbl>   <int>     <dbl>
1 Belgium Europe     1952    68   8730405     8343.
2 Belgium Europe     1957    69.2 8989111     9715.
3 Belgium Europe     1962    70.2 9218400    10991.
4 Belgium Europe     1967    70.9 9556500    13149.
5 Belgium Europe     1972    71.4 9709100    16672.
6 Belgium Europe     1977    72.8 9821800    19118.
7 Belgium Europe     1982    73.9 9856303    20980.
8 Belgium Europe     1987    75.4 9870200    22526.

Filter with multiple conditions

# Filter with multiple conditions
gapminder %>% 
  filter(country == "Oman" & year > 1980 & year <= 2000) %>% 
  head(8)
# A tibble: 4 × 6
  country continent  year lifeExp     pop gdpPercap
  <fct>   <fct>     <int>   <dbl>   <int>     <dbl>
1 Oman    Asia       1982    62.7 1301048    12955.
2 Oman    Asia       1987    67.7 1593882    18115.
3 Oman    Asia       1992    71.2 1915208    18617.
4 Oman    Asia       1997    72.5 2283635    19702.

Filter using %in% operator

# Filter using %in% operator
scandinavia <- c("Denmark", "Iceland", "Norway", "Sweden", "Finland")
res <- gapminder %>% filter(country %in% scandinavia)
head(res)
# A tibble: 6 × 6
  country continent  year lifeExp     pop gdpPercap
  <fct>   <fct>     <int>   <dbl>   <int>     <dbl>
1 Denmark Europe     1952    70.8 4334000     9692.
2 Denmark Europe     1957    71.8 4487831    11100.
3 Denmark Europe     1962    72.4 4646899    13583.
4 Denmark Europe     1967    73.0 4838800    15937.
5 Denmark Europe     1972    73.5 4991596    18866.
6 Denmark Europe     1977    74.7 5088419    20423.

Combining Select and Filter

Combine select() and filter() to extract specific data.

uk_gdp <- gapminder %>%
  filter(country == "United Kingdom") %>%
  select(country, year, gdpPercap)
head(uk_gdp)
# A tibble: 6 × 3
  country         year gdpPercap
  <fct>          <int>     <dbl>
1 United Kingdom  1952     9980.
2 United Kingdom  1957    11283.
3 United Kingdom  1962    12477.
4 United Kingdom  1967    14143.
5 United Kingdom  1972    15895.
6 United Kingdom  1977    17429.

Mutating Data

Use mutate() to create new columns and transmute() to keep only new columns.

Using Mutate to add new columns

# Add new columns
gapminder %>%  
  mutate(pop_million = pop / 1000000,
         countName = substring(country, 1, 3)) %>%
  head()
# A tibble: 6 × 8
  country     continent  year lifeExp      pop gdpPercap pop_million countName
  <fct>       <fct>     <int>   <dbl>    <int>     <dbl>       <dbl> <chr>    
1 Afghanistan Asia       1952    28.8  8425333      779.        8.43 Afg      
2 Afghanistan Asia       1957    30.3  9240934      821.        9.24 Afg      
3 Afghanistan Asia       1962    32.0 10267083      853.       10.3  Afg      
4 Afghanistan Asia       1967    34.0 11537966      836.       11.5  Afg      
5 Afghanistan Asia       1972    36.1 13079460      740.       13.1  Afg      
6 Afghanistan Asia       1977    38.4 14880372      786.       14.9  Afg

Using Mutate to Keep only new columns

# Keep only new columns
gapminder %>% transmute(total_years = lifeExp * pop) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 1
  total_years
        <dbl>
1  242658016.
2  280296010.
3  328515855.
4  392521603.
5  472011552.
6  571971739.

Using Mutate to Classify GDP per capita

# Classify GDP per capita
gapminder %>%
  mutate(GDP_Category = ifelse(gdpPercap > 10000, "High", "Low")) %>%
  select(country, year, gdpPercap, GDP_Category) %>%
  head(10)
# A tibble: 10 × 4
   country      year gdpPercap GDP_Category
   <fct>       <int>     <dbl> <chr>       
 1 Afghanistan  1952      779. Low         
 2 Afghanistan  1957      821. Low         
 3 Afghanistan  1962      853. Low         
 4 Afghanistan  1967      836. Low         
 5 Afghanistan  1972      740. Low         
 6 Afghanistan  1977      786. Low         
 7 Afghanistan  1982      978. Low         
 8 Afghanistan  1987      852. Low         
 9 Afghanistan  1992      649. Low         
10 Afghanistan  1997      635. Low

Arranging Data

Sort data using arrange().

Sort in ascending order

# Sort in ascending order
gapminder %>% arrange(year, gdpPercap) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 6
  country       continent  year lifeExp      pop gdpPercap
  <fct>         <fct>     <int>   <dbl>    <int>     <dbl>
1 Lesotho       Africa     1952    42.1   748747      299.
2 Guinea-Bissau Africa     1952    32.5   580653      300.
3 Eritrea       Africa     1952    35.9  1438760      329.
4 Myanmar       Asia       1952    36.3 20092996      331 
5 Burundi       Africa     1952    39.0  2445618      339.
6 Ethiopia      Africa     1952    34.1 20860941      362.

Sort in descending order

# Sort in descending order
gapminder %>% arrange(year, desc(gdpPercap)) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 6
  country       continent  year lifeExp       pop gdpPercap
  <fct>         <fct>     <int>   <dbl>     <int>     <dbl>
1 Kuwait        Asia       1952    55.6    160000   108382.
2 Switzerland   Europe     1952    69.6   4815000    14734.
3 United States Americas   1952    68.4 157553000    13990.
4 Canada        Americas   1952    68.8  14785584    11367.
5 New Zealand   Oceania    1952    69.4   1994794    10557.
6 Norway        Europe     1952    72.7   3327728    10095.

Summarizing Data

Compute summary statistics with summarise().

scandinavia <- c("Denmark", "Iceland", "Norway", "Sweden", "Finland")
gapminder %>% 
  filter(year == 1982 & country %in% scandinavia) %>% 
  summarise(n_obs = n(),
            total_pop = sum(pop),
            mean_life_exp = mean(lifeExp),
            range_life_exp = max(lifeExp) - min(lifeExp))
# A tibble: 1 × 4
  n_obs total_pop mean_life_exp range_life_exp
  <int>     <int>         <dbl>          <dbl>
1     5  22618787          75.7           2.44

Grouping Data

Group data with group_by() for per-group summaries.

gapminder %>% 
  group_by(year) %>% 
  summarise(n_obs = n(),
            n_countries = n_distinct(country),
            total_pop = sum(pop),
            mean_life_exp = mean(lifeExp),
            range_life_exp = max(lifeExp) - min(lifeExp))
# A tibble: 12 × 6
    year n_obs n_countries  total_pop mean_life_exp range_life_exp
   <int> <int>       <int>      <dbl>         <dbl>          <dbl>
 1  1952   142         142 2406957150          49.1           43.9
 2  1957   142         142 2664404580          51.5           43.1
 3  1962   142         142 2899782974          53.6           41.7
 4  1967   142         142 3217478384          55.7           40.1
 5  1972   142         142 3576977158          57.6           39.3
 6  1977   142         142 3930045807          59.6           44.9
 7  1982   142         142 4289436840          61.5           38.7
 8  1987   142         142 4691477418          63.2           38.8
 9  1992   142         142 5110710260          64.2           55.8
10  1997   142         142 5515204472          65.0           44.6
11  2002   142         142 5886977579          65.7           42.8
12  2007   142         142 6251013179          67.0           43.0

Joining Data

Perform joins using inner_join(), left_join(), right_join(), and full_join().

Example data for joining

# Create sample datasets
employees <- data.frame(
  id = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie", "David"),
  age = c(25, 30, 22, 28)
)
# Create sample datasets
departments <- data.frame(
  id = c(2, 3, 5, 6),
  department = c("HR", "Finance", "IT", "Sales")
)

Inner join

# Perform inner join
employees %>% inner_join(departments, by = "id")
  id    name age department
1  2     Bob  30         HR
2  3 Charlie  22    Finance

left join

# Perform left join
employees %>% left_join(departments, by = "id")
  id    name age department
1  1   Alice  25       <NA>
2  2     Bob  30         HR
3  3 Charlie  22    Finance
4  4   David  28       <NA>

right join

# Perform right join
employees %>% right_join(departments, by = "id")
  id    name age department
1  2     Bob  30         HR
2  3 Charlie  22    Finance
3  5    <NA>  NA         IT
4  6    <NA>  NA      Sales

full join

# Perform full join
employees %>% full_join(departments, by = "id")
  id    name age department
1  1   Alice  25       <NA>
2  2     Bob  30         HR
3  3 Charlie  22    Finance
4  4   David  28       <NA>
5  5    <NA>  NA         IT
6  6    <NA>  NA      Sales

Reading and Writing Files

Use readr to read and write delimited files, and openxlsx for Excel files.

Write to a file

# Write to a file
write_delim(res, "./methods_for_data_scince/applied_R/episodes/data/gapminder2.txt", delim = "\t")

Write to Excel

# Write to Excel
write.xlsx(res, file = "./methods_for_data_scince/applied_R/episodes/data//gapm3.xlsx", sheetName = "gap", colNames = TRUE, rowNames = TRUE)

Read from Excel

# Read from Excel
xl <- read.xlsx("./methods_for_data_scince/applied_R/episodes/data//gapm3.xlsx", sheet = "gap")

Tidying Data with tidyr

Handle missing values using tidyr.

Sample data with missing values

# Sample data with missing values
df <- data.frame(
  Name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie", NA, "Eve"),
  Age = c(25, NA, 30, 28, 22),
  Score = c(80, 90, NA, 75, 95)
)

Remove rows with any NA

# Remove rows with any NA
df_clean <- drop_na(df)

Remove rows with NA in specific column

# Remove rows with NA in specific column
df_clean_age <- drop_na(df, Age)

Replace NA values

# Replace NA values
df_filled <- df %>% replace_na(list(Age = 0, Score = 50, Name = "Unknown"))

String Manipulation with stringr

Perform string operations using stringr.

Split string with stringr

# Split string
text <- "Hello world, welcome to R programming!"
words <- str_split(text, " ")
print(words)
[[1]]
[1] "Hello"        "world,"       "welcome"      "to"           "R"           
[6] "programming!"

Here we split the sentence up into a list of words

Access specific word with stringr

# Access specific word
print(words[[1]][3])
[1] "welcome"

We can print specific works in the list

Extract substring with stringr

kirtqsdorsbh#| echo: true #| echo: true # Extract substring sub_text <- str_sub(text, 7, 11) print(sub_text)

we can extract a substring

Replace substring with stringr

# Replace substring
str_sub(text, 1, 5) <- "Hi"
print(text)
[1] "Hi world, welcome to R programming!"

and we can replace a substring directly

Visualizations with ggplot2

Create visualizations using ggplot2.

Scatter plot with ggplot2

# Basic scatter plot
ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = lifeExp, y = gdpPercap)) +
  geom_point()

Bar plot with ggplot2

# Bar plot
ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = continent)) +
  geom_bar()

Histogram plot with ggplot2

# Histogram
ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = lifeExp)) +
  geom_histogram()
`stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.

Box plot with ggplot2

# Box plot
ggplot(gapminder, aes(x = continent, y = lifeExp)) +
  geom_boxplot()

Violin plot with ggplot2

# Violin plot
ggplot(gapminder, aes(x = continent, y = gdpPercap)) +
  geom_violin()

Scatter plot with color aesthetic with ggplot2

# Scatter plot with color aesthetic
ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = lifeExp, y = gdpPercap, color = continent)) +
  geom_point()

Scatter plot with custom color

# Scatter plot with custom color
ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = lifeExp, y = gdpPercap)) +
  geom_point(color = "red")

Scatter plot with smooth line

# Scatter plot with smooth line
ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = lifeExp, y = gdpPercap)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(alpha = 0.5)
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'

Faceted plot in ggplot2

# Faceted plot
ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = lifeExp, y = gdpPercap)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~continent)
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'

Customized axes

# Customized axes
ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = lifeExp, y = gdpPercap)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(alpha = 0.5) +
  xlab("Life Expectancy") +
  ylab("GDP Per Capita") +
  xlim(0, 70) +
  ylim(0, 100000)
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'
Warning: Removed 496 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_smooth()`).
Warning: Removed 496 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_point()`).

Box plot with customized y-axis

# Box plot with customized y-axis
ggplot(gapminder, aes(x = continent, y = lifeExp)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Life Expectancy (Years)",
                     limits = c(10, 90),
                     breaks = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90))

Box plot with customized x-axis

# Box plot with customized x-axis
ggplot(gapminder, aes(x = continent, y = lifeExp)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Continent",
                   labels = c("Africa" = "AF", "Americas" = "AM",
                              "Asia" = "AS", "Europe" = "EU", "Oceania" = "OC"))

Color gradient scale

# Color gradient scale
ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = gdpPercap, y = lifeExp, color = pop)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red")

Discrete color scale

# Discrete color scale
ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = gdpPercap, y = lifeExp, color = continent)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1f78b4", "#33a02c", "#e31a1c", "#ff7f00", "#6a3d9a"))

Plot with color palette

# Plot with color palette
ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = lifeExp, y = gdpPercap)) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = continent), size = 5, shape = 21) +
  geom_smooth(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_minimal()
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'

Box plot with theme_classic

# Box plot with theme_classic
ggplot(gapminder, aes(x = continent, y = lifeExp)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_classic()

Box plot with jitter

# Box plot with jitter
ggplot(gapminder, aes(x = continent, y = lifeExp)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.colour = "hotpink") +
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitter(width = 0.1, height = 0), alpha = 1/4)

Combined violin and box plot

# Combined violin and box plot
ggplot(gapminder) +
  geom_violin(aes(x = continent, y = gdpPercap), scale = "width") +
  geom_boxplot(aes(x = continent, y = gdpPercap), width = 0.1)

Multiple plots with gridExtra

# Multiple plots with gridExtra
p1 <- ggplot(gapminder) + geom_boxplot(aes(x = continent, y = gdpPercap, fill = continent))
p2 <- ggplot(gapminder) + geom_violin(aes(x = continent, y = gdpPercap, fill = continent))
grid.arrange(p1, p2, nrow = 1)

Marginal histogram

# Marginal histogram
p <- ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = lifeExp, y = gdpPercap, fill = continent)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
ggMarginal(p, type = "histogram")

Saving Plots

Save a plot to a file using ggsave.

p1 <- ggplot(data = gapminder, aes(x = lifeExp, y = gdpPercap)) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = continent), size = 5, shape = 21) +
  geom_smooth(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_minimal()
print(getwd())
[1] "/Users/svztg/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-TheUniversityofNottingham/Digital_Research/Source_Control/Data Science and Digital Skills/training_site/methods_for_data_science/applied_R/episodes"
ggsave("myplot.png", plot = p1)
Saving 7 x 5 in image
`geom_smooth()` using method = 'gam' and formula = 'y ~ s(x, bs = "cs")'

Exercise Solutions

Below are the solutions to the provided exercises.

Exercise 1: Select year, lifeExp, and country

gapminder %>% select(year, lifeExp, country) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 3
   year lifeExp country    
  <int>   <dbl> <fct>      
1  1952    28.8 Afghanistan
2  1957    30.3 Afghanistan
3  1962    32.0 Afghanistan
4  1967    34.0 Afghanistan
5  1972    36.1 Afghanistan
6  1977    38.4 Afghanistan

Exercise 2: Select all columns except year, lifeExp, and country

gapminder %>% select(-year, -lifeExp, -country) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 3
  continent      pop gdpPercap
  <fct>        <int>     <dbl>
1 Asia       8425333      779.
2 Asia       9240934      821.
3 Asia      10267083      853.
4 Asia      11537966      836.
5 Asia      13079460      740.
6 Asia      14880372      786.

Exercise 3: Filter for year 2002

gapminder %>% filter(year == 2002) %>% head()
# A tibble: 6 × 6
  country     continent  year lifeExp      pop gdpPercap
  <fct>       <fct>     <int>   <dbl>    <int>     <dbl>
1 Afghanistan Asia       2002    42.1 25268405      727.
2 Albania     Europe     2002    75.7  3508512     4604.
3 Algeria     Africa     2002    71.0 31287142     5288.
4 Angola      Africa     2002    41.0 10866106     2773.
5 Argentina   Americas   2002    74.3 38331121     8798.
6 Australia   Oceania    2002    80.4 19546792    30688.

Exercise 4: Store and display data for Asian countries

gapminder_asia <- gapminder %>% filter(continent == "Asia")
head(gapminder_asia)
# A tibble: 6 × 6
  country     continent  year lifeExp      pop gdpPercap
  <fct>       <fct>     <int>   <dbl>    <int>     <dbl>
1 Afghanistan Asia       1952    28.8  8425333      779.
2 Afghanistan Asia       1957    30.3  9240934      821.
3 Afghanistan Asia       1962    32.0 10267083      853.
4 Afghanistan Asia       1967    34.0 11537966      836.
5 Afghanistan Asia       1972    36.1 13079460      740.
6 Afghanistan Asia       1977    38.4 14880372      786.

Exercise 5: Compare Ireland and Brazil in 1977

# Ireland
gapminder %>% filter(year == 1977, country == "Ireland")
# A tibble: 1 × 6
  country continent  year lifeExp     pop gdpPercap
  <fct>   <fct>     <int>   <dbl>   <int>     <dbl>
1 Ireland Europe     1977    72.0 3271900    11151.
# Brazil
gapminder %>% filter(year == 1977, country == "Brazil")
# A tibble: 1 × 6
  country continent  year lifeExp       pop gdpPercap
  <fct>   <fct>     <int>   <dbl>     <int>     <dbl>
1 Brazil  Americas   1977    61.5 114313951     6660.

Exercise 6: African countries in 2012 with specific criteria

df <- gapminder %>%
  filter(continent == "Africa" & year == 2012 & pop <= 3 & lifeExp >= 70) %>%
  select(country, continent)
df
# A tibble: 0 × 2
# ℹ 2 variables: country <fct>, continent <fct>

Exercise 7: Lowest life expectancy

gapminder %>% arrange(lifeExp) %>% head(1)
# A tibble: 1 × 6
  country continent  year lifeExp     pop gdpPercap
  <fct>   <fct>     <int>   <dbl>   <int>     <dbl>
1 Rwanda  Africa     1992    23.6 7290203      737.

Exercise 8: Highest life expectancy

gapminder %>% arrange(desc(lifeExp)) %>% head(1)
# A tibble: 1 × 6
  country continent  year lifeExp       pop gdpPercap
  <fct>   <fct>     <int>   <dbl>     <int>     <dbl>
1 Japan   Asia       2007    82.6 127467972    31656.

Exercise 9: Shortest life expectancy in the Americas in 1962

gapminder %>%
  mutate(lifeExpMonths = 12 * lifeExp) %>%
  filter(year == 1962, continent == "Americas") %>%
  arrange(lifeExpMonths) %>% head(1)
# A tibble: 1 × 7
  country continent  year lifeExp     pop gdpPercap lifeExpMonths
  <fct>   <fct>     <int>   <dbl>   <int>     <dbl>         <dbl>
1 Bolivia Americas   1962    43.4 3593918     2181.          521.

Exercise 10: Summary statistics for life expectancy

gapminder %>%
  summarise(
    lifeExp_min = min(lifeExp),
    lifeExp_max = max(lifeExp),
    lifeExp_mean = mean(lifeExp)
  )
# A tibble: 1 × 3
  lifeExp_min lifeExp_max lifeExp_mean
        <dbl>       <dbl>        <dbl>
1        23.6        82.6         59.5

Exercise 11: Life expectancy by year

gapminder %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    lifeExp_min = min(lifeExp),
    lifeExp_max = max(lifeExp),
    lifeExp_mean = mean(lifeExp)
  )
# A tibble: 12 × 4
    year lifeExp_min lifeExp_max lifeExp_mean
   <int>       <dbl>       <dbl>        <dbl>
 1  1952        28.8        72.7         49.1
 2  1957        30.3        73.5         51.5
 3  1962        32.0        73.7         53.6
 4  1967        34.0        74.2         55.7
 5  1972        35.4        74.7         57.6
 6  1977        31.2        76.1         59.6
 7  1982        38.4        77.1         61.5
 8  1987        39.9        78.7         63.2
 9  1992        23.6        79.4         64.2
10  1997        36.1        80.7         65.0
11  2002        39.2        82           65.7
12  2007        39.6        82.6         67.0

Exercise 12: Explore join statements

Already demonstrated in the “Joining Data” section above.

Exercise 13: Read a file

This requires a file like gapminder.xlsx. Assuming it’s available:

xl <- read.xlsx("./methods_for_data_scince/applied_R/episodes/data/gapm3.xlsx", sheet = "gap")
head(xl)

Exercise 14: Get and split working directory path

# Get working directory
wd <- getwd()
# Split by appropriate delimiter
path_parts <- str_split(wd, "/")[[1]]
# Fetch last element
last_element <- path_parts[length(path_parts)]
print(last_element)
[1] "episodes"

Conclusion

This document covers key tidyverse functionalities and ggplot2 visualizations using the gapminder dataset. It includes data manipulation, file handling, string manipulation, and various plotting techniques, along with solutions to the exercises.

To download this R Markdown file, save it with a .Rmd extension and open it in RStudio or another R Markdown-compatible environment.

Cookie Preferences
